rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into the intended target component or implementation while preserving AADL architecture
Place component type annexes in valid component type scopes and implementation annexes in valid implementation scopes
Preserve the authoritative source of component names, ports, directions, data types, subcomponents, and connections from the input AADL model
Use case-provided thresholds and legal visible feature names for input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees
generic_rules: Maintain the original AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Ensure that AGREE annex blocks are placed in valid scopes according to AADL syntax rules
Use only visible identifiers that are explicitly provided in the current visible identifiers list
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Component type annexes are the normal location for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Implementation-level bindings should be placed in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in a component type annex
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants for them
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers for range comparisons or 0/1 equality checks
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
